title: MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship
summary: The MPhil/PhD Studentship – Pan-African Frontiers: Reparative Futures and Global Order at SOAS University of London offers a fully funded doctoral opportunity for candidates interested in Pan-Africanism, global justice, reparations, and international governance . This studentship is part of a UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship project led by Daniel Mulugeta and hosted at the SOAS Centre for Pan-African Studies (CPAS) within the Department of Politics and International Studies. Key Information Host Institution: SOAS University of London Programme: Full-time MPhil/PhD in Politics and International Studies Academic Year: 2026/27 Funding: Full tuition fees plus maintenance stipend Minimum Stipend: £20,622 (2026/27 estimate) Duration: 3 years (full-time) Number of Awards: 1 Deadline: 15 May 2026 (12 noon UK time) Scholarship Overview The Pan-African Frontiers project explores Pan-Africanism as a transcontinental political formation linking Africa, the Caribbean, and diasporic communities. It focuses on: Global justice and reparations Historical redress for slavery and colonialism Institutional reform and global governance New forms of political agency and solidarity The research programme brings together scholars and practitioners across Africa, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America to examine how historically marginalised communities are shaping the future of global order . Scholarship Benefits Full tuition fees (Home and Overseas applicants) Maintenance stipend (minimum £20,622) Three years of doctoral funding Teaching experience through the SOAS Teaching Scholarship scheme Access to interdisciplinary research networks and global collaboration Research Areas Applicants should propose projects aligned with themes such as: Reparations and historical justice Pan-Africanism and global political order Diaspora engagement and transnational networks African and Caribbean participation in global governance Multilateralism and institutional reform Regional cooperation in Africa and the Caribbean Political responses to global inequality and structural injustice Projects may adopt political, sociological, historical, or interdisciplinary approaches and may focus on continental, regional, or diasporic contexts. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Apply for a new MPhil/PhD programme starting in 2026/27 Hold or expect a Master’s degree with merit or distinction (or international equivalent) Meet SOAS English language requirements (if applicable) Be eligible for Home or Overseas fee status Required Documents Applications must include: Personal statement (maximum 1 page) Research proposal (maximum 4 pages) CV (maximum 2 pages) One academic reference Academic transcripts and certificates Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable) Incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Step 1: Apply for the Programme All applications must include the following documents. Applications that do not contain these documents will not be considered. Personal statement explaining your motivation for applying and how your proposed research relates to the themes of the project (maximum 1 page). Research proposal outlining the proposed doctoral project (maximum 4 pages). Curriculum vitae (CV) including relevant academic background and experience (maximum 2 pages). One academic reference. Academic transcripts and degree certificates. Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable), such as IELTS or equivalent. Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship The deadline for application is 12 noon UK local time on 15 May 2026. You must apply for this scholarship via the admissions portal . You will need to have applied for a course to get login credentials for this. If you do not see the scholarship you are hoping to apply for within your admissions portal, please double-check you meet the eligibility criteria. Should you have any queries, please contact scholarships@soas.ac.uk . Selection Process Applications will be assessed based on: Academic merit Quality and relevance of research proposal Personal statement Relevant academic or professional experience Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Notification of Results Shortlisting and selection expected from June 2026 Successful candidates contacted first All applicants notified once the process is complete Contact Information Programme enquiries: dsadmissions@soas.ac.uk Scholarship enquiries: scholarships@soas.ac.uk Why This Opportunity Matters This fully funded PhD offers a strong platform for candidates seeking to contribute to global debates on justice, reparations, and institutional reform . It provides the opportunity to work within a leading research environment and engage with critical issues shaping Africa, the diaspora, and the future of global governance . Source: https://www.soas.ac.uk/study/student-life/finance/scholarships/mphil/phd-studentship-pan-african-frontiers-reparative The post MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth)
summary: The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 is a global, paid United Nations Volunteer initiative designed to expand access to employment opportunities for refugee, asylum-seeking, internally displaced, and migrant youth. Hosted by UNICEF , the programme provides a structured pathway for young people to gain professional experience and contribute meaningfully to international development. Overview of the Programme The Youth on the Move Programme offers a 12-month paid UN Volunteer assignment within UNICEF country offices. It is specifically designed to address systemic barriers that prevent displaced and migrant youth from accessing internships and entry-level roles. The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme builds on the success of the Youth on the Move Fellowship Pilot Programme —implemented by UNICEF in partnership with the Migration Youth and Children Platform. This pilot included 23 young people from refugee and displaced backgrounds to work with UNICEF between 2022 and 2024 (see the video ). While the 2024–2025 edition engaged 13 UN Volunteers with UNICEF in Cambodia, Côte d’Ivoire, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Mozambique, Poland, Somalia, Tanzania, Trinidad and Tobago, and Vietnam. The 2026 edition coincides with the International Volunteer Year (IVY) 2026 and builds on the momentum of previous cohorts. Key Benefits Participants in the programme will receive: Paid 12-month UN Volunteer placement Professional experience within UNICEF country offices Exposure to international development and humanitarian work Opportunities to build networks within the UN system Career development support and practical work experience Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Aged 20–32 years Refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced persons, or migrants Facing barriers to education-to-employment pathways due to displacement or migration Based in the country of assignment Legally eligible to work in the country of assignment Additional considerations: UNICEF promotes equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply Youth with disabilities are encouraged to apply UNICEF is committed to equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment, particularly for youth on the move, including those with disabilities. Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to support gender parity and address the heightened vulnerabilities faced by women and girls in displaced communities (see link ). Available Opportunities (2026) Current programme locations include: Uganda Sudan More opportunities may be added as the programme expands. Application Process To apply for the UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026: Register on the UN Volunteer Unified Platform Complete your profile and required documentation Review available roles and apply directly Consult guidance and application resources provided on the platform To join the 31 March information webinar on the cohort , please click here . How to apply? Visit the Unified Volunteer Platform or apply to the positions below. Register your profile by c licking on guidance and resources . Programme Vision The programme is grounded in the principle that talent is universal, but access to opportunity is not. It aims to address systemic exclusion faced by displaced youth by creating equitable access to employment pathways. Core objectives include: Breaking barriers to entry into international organizations Promoting inclusion and representation within global institutions Unlocking untapped potential among displaced and migrant youth Supporting sustainable development through youth participation The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 represents a significant opportunity for young people affected by displacement to gain paid, meaningful work experience within the United Nations system. It is both a career pathway and a strategic intervention aimed at advancing equity in global employment opportunities. CLICK FOR MORE INFO AND TO APPLY The post UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions
summary: Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026 , a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities. This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union , and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the Latin America and Caribbean Cyber Competence Centre (LAC4) . About the Programme HerCyberTracks is a five-month blended learning programme that combines virtual training, self-paced learning, mentorship, and in-person regional sessions. It is designed to support women working in cybersecurity-related roles to accelerate their careers, deepen technical expertise, and build strong international networks . Programme Structure Participants will engage in: Live online sessions with industry experts Self-paced learning modules In-person regional training sessions Personalised mentorship Inspirational webinars and keynote sessions This hybrid format ensures both flexibility and immersive learning experiences. Specialised Tracks Applicants can choose one of four tailored tracks: Policy and Diplomacy Incident Response Criminal Justice Cyber or AI Each track is designed to provide practical knowledge and leadership skills relevant to the evolving cybersecurity landscape. What You’ll Gain Participants will benefit from: Advanced cybersecurity and policy skills Access to global experts and mentors International networking opportunities Exposure to cross-regional collaboration Leadership development to influence policy and systems Who Should Apply This programme targets women professionals working in: Public sector institutions Critical infrastructure organisations Cybersecurity, policy, or related technical fields Eligible Regions Applications are open to candidates from: African Union member states Western Balkans Eastern Partnership countries (selected) Latin America and the Caribbean Indo-Pacific region Programme Duration Duration: 5 months (2026 cohort) Application Deadline Applications close on April 19, 2026 . How to Apply Apply here. For more Training Programs from OFY, Click here. title: Comprehensive Security Fellowship 2027–2028 in Germany: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers & Practitioners
summary: Applications are now open for the Fellowship on “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience” —a prestigious programme designed for professionals and researchers working on global security, international relations, and resilience. Hosted in Bonn , Germany , this fellowship provides a unique platform to develop innovative ideas addressing today’s evolving geopolitical challenges. About the Fellowship This programme brings together experts from diverse backgrounds to explore pressing issues in international politics, security, and technological resilience. Participants will have the opportunity to work on forward-thinking projects that contribute to academic research and real-world policy solutions. Fellowship Tracks Applicants can choose between two distinct tracks depending on their background and goals: Academic Track Designed for researchers and scholars, this track focuses on developing research-driven projects that engage with current academic debates in security and international relations. Practitioner’s Track Tailored for professionals, this track emphasizes policy-oriented projects grounded in real-world experience and practical application. What You’ll Gain This fellowship offers a strong platform for professional and academic growth. Key benefits include: Opportunity to develop impactful research or policy projects Engagement with global experts and peers Exposure to international security and policy discussions Access to a collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment Enhanced career opportunities in academia, policy, or international organizations Programme Details Location: Bonn, Germany Duration: April 2027 – March 2028 Why You Should Apply As global challenges become more complex, there is a growing need for innovative thinking in security and resilience. This fellowship provides the space, resources, and network to develop ideas that can influence policy and academic discourse on a global scale. Application Deadline Applications close on May 31, 2026 . How to Apply Apply here. For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here. title: A+ Alliance Gender & AI Innovation Collective 2026: Funding & Tech Support for Women-Led Organisations in Africa
summary: Applications are now open for Cohort 2 of the A+ Alliance Gender AI Innovation Collective (GAIC) —a unique programme designed to support women-led organisations across sub-Saharan Africa in scaling their impact through technology. This initiative is co-led by Code for Africa (CfA) and Women at the Table , with support from the International Development Research Centre (IDRC) and the FCDO Services under the Artificial Intelligence for Development (AI4D) programme. About the Programme The Gender AI Innovation Collective (GAIC) is designed to help women-led organisations build and scale tech-driven solutions that address critical challenges affecting women and girls. Rather than just funding ideas, the programme provides hands-on support to develop, refine, and scale technology solutions that can drive real impact. What the Programme Offers Selected organisations will benefit from a structured support system that combines innovation, mentorship, and technology development. Key benefits include: Technical support to build and scale digital solutions Access to expert mentorship and advisory Opportunities to strengthen organisational capacity Collaboration with a network of like-minded organisations Support in leveraging AI and technology for social impact Focus Areas The programme is looking for organisations actively working to address: Gender-based violence Economic inequality Healthcare access Education for women and girls Solutions that use or can integrate technology—especially AI—are strongly encouraged. Programme Details Application Window: March 5 – April 25, 2026 Programme Start Date: May 13, 2026 Format: Fully online Time Commitment: Approximately 4 hours per week Why You Should Apply Women-led organisations often face barriers in accessing technology, funding, and scaling opportunities. This programme is designed to bridge that gap by providing the tools and support needed to expand impact. If your organisation is working to improve the lives of women and girls, this is a powerful opportunity to grow your solution and reach more communities. How to Apply Apply here. For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Peter J. Rappa Resilient & Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i
summary: Applications are now open for the Peter J. Rappa Resilient and Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026 , a unique summer programme designed for students passionate about sustainability, climate adaptation, and coastal resilience. This impactful fellowship is supported by the University of Hawai‘i Sea Grant College Program and offers participants the chance to work on real-world projects across Molokaʻi and Oʻahu , Hawaii , United States . About the Fellowship The Rappa Fellowship is a 10-week summer programme that equips students with hands-on experience in building sustainable coastal communities. Fellows work on projects that bridge the gap between research and practical solutions, contributing to climate resilience, coastal planning, and community development. What You’ll Gain This fellowship offers both financial support and practical experience in sustainability-focused work. Key benefits include: A stipend of $8,000 Hands-on experience in climate adaptation and coastal resilience Exposure to real-world sustainability and planning projects Opportunities to integrate traditional knowledge into modern solutions Professional development in research, communication, and policy Focus Areas Fellows will work across key areas such as: Sustainable coastal communities Climate adaptation and resilience Coastal smart growth and hazard mitigation Environmental communication and planning Programme Details Duration: 10 weeks (June – August 2026) Location: Molokaʻi and Oʻahu, Hawai‘i Who Should Apply This programme is ideal for students and recent graduates interested in sustainability, environmental science, or coastal development. Applicants must: Be full-time undergraduate or graduate students, or recent graduates Be a U.S. citizen or national Demonstrate interest in sustainability, climate, or coastal systems Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit: Goal Statement (max 2 pages) Resume (max 2 pages) Unofficial transcripts Contact information for two references Key Dates Informational Webinar: March 27, 2026 @ 11 AM HST Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (5 PM HST) How to Apply Start your application here. Rappa Resilient Sustainable Coasts Fellowship 2026: $8,000 Summer Opportunity in Hawai‘i appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C.
summary: Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship —a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the U.S. legislative environment. This scholarship not only provides financial assistance but also opens doors to valuable networking opportunities and mentorship from professionals serving on the WCSF Board. It is a strategic initiative aimed at increasing the representation and participation of women—especially those from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds—in the legislative workforce. About the Scholarship The WCSF Scholarship is dedicated to broadening the voices and experiences of women on Capitol Hill . Recognizing that internships often serve as a critical entry point into full-time roles in government and policy, the program seeks to strengthen the pipeline by supporting women at the very beginning of their careers. A key focus of the scholarship is to ensure that women from middle- and low-income backgrounds are not excluded from these opportunities due to financial constraints. By reducing the burden of living and working in Washington, D.C., WCSF enables more women to contribute their perspectives and expertise to the legislative process. Scholarship Benefits Recipients of the WCSF Scholarship will gain: Financial support to help cover expenses during their Capitol Hill internship Professional networking opportunities with leaders and policymakers Career mentorship and guidance from members of the WCSF Board Exposure to the inner workings of the U.S. legislative system Application Deadline and Process Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Interview Notification: Finalists will be contacted to schedule interviews with the selection committee Final Award Announcement: No later than May 24, 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , meaning early submission is highly encouraged to increase your chances of selection. Why This Scholarship Matters Internships on Capitol Hill are often a stepping stone to influential careers in government, policy, and public service. However, without financial support, many qualified candidates—particularly women from underrepresented backgrounds—may be unable to pursue these opportunities. The WCSF Scholarship plays a crucial role in leveling the playing field , ensuring that talent and passion—not financial limitations—determine who gets a seat at the table in shaping public policy. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Have applied for an internship with: A Member, Delegate, or Resident Commissioner of Congress, OR A U.S. Senate or House committee, caucus, or leadership office in Washington, D.C. Provide a confirmation letter of the internship before receiving funding Identify as a woman Be at least 18 years old Hold a diploma from an accredited high school Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors: Demonstrated financial need , including expenses related to the internship Alignment between the internship and the applicant’s academic and career goals Applications for internships with significant responsibility and impact Overall quality of application materials , including resume, essays, and references Academic performance and track record Terms and Conditions Recipients of the scholarship are required to: Complete the full internship as described in their application Provide accurate and truthful information, particularly regarding financial need Failure to meet these requirements may result in forfeiture or repayment of scholarship funds . Exceptions will only be made in rare and exceptional circumstances, as determined by the WCSF Board of Directors. Additionally, the scholarship funds are non-transferable and can only be used for the internship specified in the application. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline of April 15, 2026. Since applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, applying early is strongly recommended. APPLY HERE By supporting aspiring women leaders at the start of their careers, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation is helping to build a more inclusive, representative, and effective legislative workforce. title: Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems
summary: In celebration of International Girls in ICT Day 2026 , the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has announced an exciting global opportunity for young women to participate in a virtual e-mentorship program focused on artificial intelligence (AI) and its role in agrifood systems. This initiative is designed to bring together aspiring young women, experienced mentors, and global stakeholders to engage in meaningful discussions about the future of technology and its impact on food systems worldwide. About the Initiative The E-Mentorship program is part of FAO’s broader efforts to address the digital gender divide by equipping girls and young women with the knowledge, confidence, and networks needed to thrive in technology-driven fields. Through this global online event, participants will explore both the challenges and opportunities that AI presents in transforming agrifood systems. Agrifood systems are increasingly being shaped by digital innovations, and AI is playing a critical role in improving agricultural productivity, sustainability, and resilience. However, women and girls remain underrepresented in these technological advancements. This mentorship program seeks to bridge that gap by encouraging greater participation and ensuring that girls are not only beneficiaries of innovation but also active contributors to its development. Event Theme and Objectives The 2026 edition of the event will take place on April 23 under the theme: “AI for Development: Girls Shaping the Digital Future.” This theme underscores the importance of inclusive innovation and highlights the vital role that girls and young women play in accelerating the adoption and ethical use of AI technologies. It emphasizes that empowering girls in ICT is not only a matter of equality but also a necessity for sustainable global development. The key objectives of the event include: Promoting awareness of AI applications in agrifood systems Encouraging girls to pursue careers and opportunities in ICT and related fields Providing mentorship and role models from diverse professional backgrounds Facilitating knowledge exchange between participants and experts Strengthening global efforts to close the digital gender gap Structure of the E-Mentorship Session A central component of this initiative is a two-hour virtual mentorship session tailored specifically for young women interested in AI and agrifood systems. This interactive session will feature: Expert Mentors: Professionals from the private sector, academia, and United Nations agencies will participate as mentors. These individuals bring a wealth of experience in AI, agriculture, and digital innovation. Career Insights: Mentors will share their personal career journeys, including the challenges they faced, the milestones they achieved, and the lessons they learned along the way. Aspirational Guidance: Participants will gain valuable advice on how to navigate educational and career pathways in technology and agrifood sectors. Interactive Q A Session: Attendees will have the opportunity to ask questions, seek guidance, and engage directly with experts. The session is designed to be highly engaging and participatory, ensuring that attendees not only listen but actively contribute to discussions. Certification and Participation Benefits All participants who successfully attend and complete the mentorship session will receive a certificate of participation. This certificate serves as recognition of their engagement and commitment to learning about AI applications in agrifood systems. Beyond certification, participants will benefit from: Exposure to global perspectives on AI and agriculture Opportunities to network with professionals and peers Increased awareness of career pathways in emerging technological fields Enhanced confidence to participate in digital innovation spaces Eligibility Criteria FAO invites applications from young women around the world who meet the following requirements: Must be female and aged between 18 and 25 years Demonstrated interest in technology, agriculture, or the intersection of both Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a post-secondary institution (university or vocational program) Academic background in STEM or agriculture is advantageous but not mandatory Strong motivation to learn about AI applications in agrifood systems Commitment to attend the full mentorship session and actively participate in all activities Working proficiency in English Willingness to provide feedback after the session to support the improvement of future programs Application Process and Deadline Interested candidates are encouraged to apply by April 5, 2026 . Early registration is recommended due to the global nature of the program and the potential for high demand. Click here to Register Applicants should ensure they meet all eligibility criteria and are available to attend the full session on April 23, 2026. Advancing Inclusion in the Digital Age This initiative reflects FAO’s commitment to fostering inclusive digital transformation in agrifood systems. By investing in the next generation of female leaders in ICT, the organization aims to create a more equitable and innovative future. Programs like this not only provide knowledge and mentorship but also inspire confidence and ambition among young women. As AI continues to reshape industries worldwide, ensuring that girls are equipped to participate meaningfully in this transformation is essential. The E-Mentorship for Girls in ICT Day 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for young women to gain insights, build networks, and take a step forward in shaping the digital future of agrifood systems. title: Unlock New Markets: Apply for the AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026
summary: African agribusinesses are at the heart of the continent’s economic transformation. If you are an export-ready Small or Medium Enterprise (SME) operating within the agri-food value chain, this is your opportunity to scale beyond borders and tap into regional and international markets through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). The AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026 is designed to help ambitious businesses expand their reach, strengthen their export capabilities, and build meaningful trade partnerships across Africa and with global markets like Switzerland. What Is the Programme About? This initiative is a targeted business support programme aimed at empowering agribusiness SMEs to leverage AfCFTA opportunities. A total of 30 high-potential businesses will be selected through a competitive process to receive tailored support that enhances their readiness for cross-border trade. Key Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will gain access to a comprehensive support package, including: Specialized Training on AfCFTA Opportunities Understand how to navigate and benefit from Africa’s largest free trade agreement. Market Expansion Support Position your business to enter new regional and international markets. Packaging and Labelling Assistance Improve your product presentation to meet international standards. Business Matchmaking Buyer Connections Engage directly with potential buyers through curated B2B sessions. Access to Trade Fairs and Market Linkage Events Showcase your products and connect with key industry players. Tailored Coaching by Sales Experts Develop effective go-to-market strategies with expert guidance. Participation in AfCFTA Flagship Events Gain visibility and network at high-level continental trade events. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for agribusiness SMEs that: Operate within agriculture or food value chains Are export-ready or preparing to scale internationally Are committed to actively participating in programme activities Seek to explore new trade and partnership opportunities Women-led and youth-led enterprises are strongly encouraged to apply. Programme Impact By participating in this programme, SMEs will not only strengthen their competitiveness but also gain the tools and networks needed to thrive in the evolving African trade landscape. With AfCFTA unlocking a market of over 1.3 billion people, this initiative positions your business at the forefront of continental trade integration. Application Deadline All interested applicants must submit their Expression of Interest before: 6 April 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is highly recommended. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Confidentiality Notice All submitted information will be handled with strict confidentiality and used solely for analytical and programme evaluation purposes. Final Thoughts This is more than just a training programme—it is a gateway to scaling your agribusiness across Africa and beyond. If you are ready to grow, connect, and compete on a larger stage, this opportunity could be your next big step. title: eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 in San Jose: High-Paying Opportunity for U.S. University Students
summary: The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a highly competitive, full-time internship opportunity designed for undergraduate students in the United States who are eager to gain hands-on experience in product management within a global technology company. This program stands out for its strong emphasis on real-world product development, cross-functional collaboration, and the integration of AI-powered tools in modern product workflows. With a structured 6-month duration and a generous hourly pay of up to $47, this co-op provides a rare opportunity to work directly on products used by millions of users worldwide while building critical skills in product strategy, execution, and analytics. About the eBay Product Management Co-op This co-op program is tailored for emerging talent, especially Gen Z students, who are interested in building digital products for a global audience. Participants are embedded within product teams and collaborate closely with engineering, design, research, and data professionals. The program emphasizes: AI-enabled product discovery and decision-making Real-world product lifecycle exposure Ownership of meaningful product outcomes Mentorship from experienced product leaders By the end of the program, participants are expected to deliver a comprehensive presentation showcasing their contributions, learnings, and strategic recommendations. Key Responsibilities During the co-op, selected candidates will: Product Discovery and Research Conduct user research and analyze customer insights Perform competitive analysis Use data to identify opportunities and define problems Product Development and Execution Define product requirements and user stories Collaborate with engineering and design teams Participate in Agile or Scrum-based workflows AI and Innovation Use AI-powered tools for prototyping, research, and iteration Accelerate workflows using automation and analytics tools Performance Tracking and Analysis Monitor product performance metrics Evaluate post-launch results Generate insights for continuous improvement Communication and Collaboration Work cross-functionally with multiple teams Present findings and updates to stakeholders Align product goals across departments Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Undergraduate students currently enrolled full-time in a U.S.-accredited university Students graduating between December 2026 and Summer 2027 Eligible Fields of Study Applicants should be pursuing degrees in: Product Management Business Administration Entrepreneurship Computer Science Engineering Economics Design or Industrial Design Related disciplines Who Should Apply This co-op is ideal for: Students passionate about product management and technology Individuals interested in building user-centric digital experiences Candidates looking to gain experience in a major global tech company Those who want to work at the intersection of AI, data, and product innovation Apply here Countries Eligible Only students enrolled in accredited universities within the United States Must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. This opportunity is not open to international applicants without U.S. work authorization. Work Authorization Requirements (Very Important) This role has strict eligibility conditions: Applicants must have U.S. work authorization Visa sponsorship is NOT provided Students on F1, CPT, OPT, or requiring H1B sponsorship are NOT eligible Required Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Experience (academic, internship, or project-based) in product management, technology, analytics, or consulting Familiarity with AI tools such as generative AI, data analysis platforms, or automation tools Strong analytical thinking and data-driven decision-making skills Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment A growth mindset with curiosity and adaptability Preferred Qualifications While not mandatory, the following will strengthen your application: Experience with Agile or Scrum methodologies Understanding of digital marketplaces or platform-based businesses Basic technical knowledge (APIs, backend systems, architectures) Experience solving ambiguous or complex problems Cross-functional collaboration experience Benefits of the Program Financial Benefits Competitive hourly pay: $42–$47 Full-time work experience over 6 months Career Development Exposure to real-world product management Mentorship from industry professionals Hands-on experience with AI-driven product tools Professional Impact Opportunity to contribute to products used globally Build a strong portfolio project Gain experience highly valued by top tech companies Location and Work Setup Primary Location: San Jose, California Work Mode: Hybrid (combination of in-office and remote work) Relocation: Candidates must be willing to relocate if not already in the area Important Dates Interview Day: April 10, 2026 (mandatory availability required) Interview Duration: 1.5 hours (virtual) Application Policy: Only one application per candidate is allowed Start Date: June 15, 2026 End Date: December 11, 2026 Applicants must be available for the scheduled interview day. No alternative dates or rescheduling options are provided, making this a strict and critical deadline. Deadline 8th April 2026 The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a premier opportunity for students aiming to break into product management. With strong compensation, hands-on experience, and exposure to cutting-edge AI tools, this program offers both professional growth and real-world impact.
